zh.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp於大約1241年出生在英國威爾斯。他於大約1259年與Mrs Andrew Beauchamp在威爾士結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp于大约1241年出生在英国威尔斯。他于大约1259年与Mrs Andrew Beauchamp在威尔士结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp was born about 1241, in Wales, United Kingdom. He married Mrs Andrew Beauchamp about 1259, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp est né environ 1241, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Mrs Andrew Beauchamp environ 1259, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp wurde um 1241 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat um 1259 in Wales Mrs Andrew Beauchamp geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp è nato il circa 1241 nel Galles, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Mrs Andrew Beauchamp circa 1259, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchampは約1241年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた。約1259年にウェールズでMrs Andrew Beauchampと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp(은)는 대략 1241년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1259년 웨일스에서 Mrs Andrew Beauchamp(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1241, em Gales, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Mrs Andrew Beauchamp aproximadamente 1259, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp родился около 1241 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Andrew Beauchamp около 1259 г. в местности Уэльс. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Andrew de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1241, en Gales, Reino Unido. Se casó con Mrs Andrew Beauchamp aproximadamente 1259, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
